description: Produced beginning in 1710, Meissen porcelain was an exclusive luxury good of its time. Around 1728, Augustus, the king of Poland, conceived of replicating the animal kingdom in porcelain for a Baroque palace. By the year of his death, more than 30 birds and almost 40 animals had been made, many by the sculptor Johann Joachim Kändler, who drew this vulture from life.
artist_info:
Meissen Porcelain Manufactory (1710–present)
Modeled by Johann Joachim Kändler (born Saxony [now Germany]
1706–1775)
Meissen, Electorate of Saxony (now Germany)
title: King Vulture
